title: Relative Clauses & Complex Sentences — Whose & Whom
objectives: Hiểu và áp dụng: whose & whom
Học 24 từ vựng theo chủ đề tuần 11
Hoàn thành 40 câu hỏi trắc nghiệm tăng dần độ khó
Bám sát mục tiêu tuần: Relative Clauses & Complex Sentences
lesson: **Relative Clauses.** Relative clauses give more **information** about a **noun** using relative **pronouns**: *who* (people), *which* (things), *that* (people or things), *where* (places), *whose* (possession) and *when* (time). A **defining** clause gives essential information and uses no commas: 'The man *who* called is my uncle.' A **non-defining** clause adds extra information and uses commas: 'My uncle, *who* lives in Hue, is a doctor.' Relative clauses help you **combine** two short sentences into one **complex** sentence: 'I read a book. It was great.' → 'The book *which* I read was great.' Practise joining sentences with *who*, *which*, *that* and *where* to **describe** people and places.
